Gillespie County Stucco Services

Star Stucco of Austin delivers premium exterior finishes and expert stucco services to homes and businesses across Gillespie County. From the charming German heritage architecture in Fredericksburg to sprawling ranch estates outside Harper, we bring the skill and attention to detail that Hill Country properties demand.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Premium Stucco for the Heart of the Hill Country

Gillespie County occupies some of the most scenic landscape in Texas, and the homes built here reflect that beauty. Fredericksburg draws visitors from across the state, but the people who live here year-round know that maintaining a property in this climate takes real effort. Summer temperatures regularly hit triple digits, spring thunderstorms deliver hail and high winds, and the rocky caliche soil creates drainage challenges that affect foundations and exterior walls alike.

Stucco has long been a natural fit for Gillespie County architecture. It complements the limestone, timber, and metal roofing materials that define Hill Country design, and when properly installed, it handles the thermal swings and moisture cycles of this region without the constant upkeep that other siding materials require. Our team understands the specific demands of this landscape and builds every project to perform under real conditions.

Stucco Services We Offer in Gillespie County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Gillespie County.

Stucco Repair

Gillespie County homes endure harsh UV rays and seasonal storms that wear down stucco over time. We repair cracked, chipped, and deteriorated surfaces using time-tested methods and premium materials that stand up to the specific weather patterns of this part of the Hill Country.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

Whether you are building a traditional German-inspired farmhouse or a contemporary ranch home, we install stucco systems engineered for Gillespie County conditions. Every installation includes proper moisture barriers, reinforced lath, and curing protocols adapted to the local climate.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

The rocky terrain and caliche subsoil around Fredericksburg and Harper cause subtle foundation shifts that crack stucco walls. We repair these cracks using flexible bonding compounds and reinforcement that allow for continued minor movement without re-cracking.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Spring storms can dump several inches of rain on Gillespie County in a matter of hours, overwhelming drainage systems and pushing water behind exterior walls. We identify the entry point, remove damaged material, treat for mold, and rebuild the stucco with improved water management details.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Fredericksburg is known for its distinctive architecture, and many homeowners here want plaster finishes that honor that tradition. We apply old-world techniques alongside modern materials, creating finishes that range from smooth Venetian to rustic hand-troweled textures with authentic Hill Country character.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS provides a lightweight, energy-efficient exterior option well suited to the extreme summer heat in Gillespie County. We install properly detailed drainage EIFS systems and repair existing installations that may be suffering from moisture intrusion or delamination.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

From Main Street boutiques and wine tasting rooms to bed-and-breakfast properties along Highway 290, Gillespie County businesses depend on attractive, durable exteriors. We work with property owners and general contractors to deliver commercial stucco projects on schedule and on budget.

Learn More

Why Gillespie County Homeowners Choose Star Stucco

Over a decade of hands-on experience with the specific soil, climate, and building styles of the Hill Country

Expertise in blending stucco with limestone, timber, and other natural materials common in Gillespie County homes

Premium finish options including hand-troweled textures that complement the area's heritage architecture

Transparent communication from first inspection through final walkthrough with no surprise charges

Meticulous attention to flashing, weep screeds, and moisture control that prevents callbacks down the road

Flexible scheduling that accommodates vacation rental turnovers and seasonal business demands in Fredericksburg

Local Expertise

We understand Gillespie County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Gillespie County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Gillespie County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Gillespie County

The combination of extreme heat, rocky terrain, and seasonal storms creates a specific set of stucco challenges for Gillespie County property owners. These are the issues we encounter most often.

Cracks

Shallow caliche and limestone bedrock beneath much of Gillespie County transmit vibration and micro-movement to foundations, producing vertical and stair-step cracks in stucco walls that worsen with each passing season if not properly addressed.

Bubbling or soft stucco

Morning fog and heavy dew in the Pedernales River valley keep certain wall surfaces damp well into the day, trapping moisture under the finish coat and causing bubbling or sponginess, particularly on homes nestled among tree cover near Stonewall and Willow City.

Water stains

Iron-rich minerals in the local soil and groundwater leave rust-colored stains on stucco surfaces, especially below window sills, at foundation transitions, and wherever water is allowed to pool or run down the wall face without proper diversion.

Window and door leaks

Older homes around Fredericksburg often lack modern flashing details at window and door penetrations. Over time, dried caulk and degraded building paper allow water to wick behind the stucco, causing hidden damage that only becomes visible when interior drywall starts to stain.

Failing patches

Handyman repairs and quick-fix patches applied without proper surface preparation peel away from the surrounding stucco, creating edges where water collects and accelerates further deterioration of the wall system.

Separation around trim

Decorative wood trim used on many Hill Country homes pulls away from adjacent stucco as the wood dries and shrinks in low-humidity summers, creating visible gaps that allow moisture and pests into the wall cavity.

Impact damage

Golf-ball-sized hail from spring supercells, wind-blown debris during severe thunderstorms, and even deer rubbing against walls leave gouges and fractures in stucco surfaces across Gillespie County properties.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Gillespie County receives some of the most intense solar radiation in Central Texas. Over the years, this relentless UV exposure bleaches colored stucco finishes unevenly, while algae and lichen take hold on shaded north-facing walls, creating a patchy appearance.

How We Work in Gillespie County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Reach out by phone or online to arrange an on-site evaluation at your Gillespie County property. We serve the entire county from Fredericksburg to Harper and everywhere in between.

2

Review scope

We conduct a thorough hands-on evaluation of your stucco, checking for moisture behind walls, examining control joints, and identifying any areas of concern. We document everything with photos and notes so you have a clear picture of your home's condition.

3

Get estimate

Based on our findings, we prepare a straightforward written proposal covering the recommended scope of work, materials, timeline, and total cost. We are happy to answer questions and will never pressure you into work you do not need.

4

Complete work

Our crew handles the project from start to finish, keeping your property tidy throughout the process. When the work is done, we walk you through every detail and make sure you are completely satisfied before we consider the job finished.
